title: GitHub Connection Validation Before Save
story: As a DataTools Pro user setting up the GitHub integration, I want the system to validate my connection credentials before saving so that I have confidence my token, repository owner, and repository name are correct.
description:
Before allowing a user to save their GitHub connection details, validate that the credentials are correct by testing the connection. This provides immediate feedback and prevents users from saving invalid configurations.

Validation checks:
1. Token validity: verify the personal access token is valid
2. Repository access: verify the repository owner and repository name exist and are accessible
3. Permissions: confirm the token has the required permissions for file operations

Error messages:
- Invalid token: clear error message indicating the token is not valid
- Invalid repo/owner: error indicating the repository or owner name is incorrect

Also update the documentation link on the settings page to point to the tutorial for creating a personal access token: https://datatoolspro.com/tutorials/github-personal-access-token/
acceptance_criteria: Connection is validated on save before credentials are stored
Invalid token shows a clear error message
Invalid repository or owner shows a specific error message
Valid connection with correct permissions saves successfully
Documentation link points to the GitHub personal access token tutorial
ux_changes: Pre-save validation with specific error messages for invalid tokens and incorrect repository/owner. Updated documentation link to the GitHub personal access token tutorial. Removed previous generic documentation.
middleware_changes: Connection validation service that checks token validity, repository existence, and permission levels via the GitHub API before allowing credential storage.
api_changes: Outbound validation calls to GitHub API to verify token, repository access, and permissions before saving connection details.
implementation_notes: Validation uses the GitHub API to check token validity first, then verifies repository access. Two distinct error states are handled: invalid token (401) and invalid repo/owner (404). The tutorial link for creating a personal access token was updated to the datatoolspro.com documentation.
comment_summary: Developer added validation checks that run before saving and show specific error messages for invalid tokens and incorrect repository/owner names. Deployed to development for review.
